zh.lifeSummary: 當John Poley於1511年出生在英國素福爾可巴德利時，他的父親Edmund Poley25歲，母親Mirabelle Garneys18歲。他於1541年與Anne Agnes Wentworth在英國英格蘭素福爾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1589年10月20日在他的家鄉享年78歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可巴德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Poley于1511年出生在英国素福爾可巴德利时，他的父亲Edmund Poley25岁，母亲Mirabelle Garneys18岁。他于1541年与Anne Agnes Wentworth在英国英格兰素福爾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1589年10月20日在他的家乡享年78岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可巴德利。

nameMeanings.name: Poley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Nord): from a variant of Polet, a vernacular pet form of the personal name Paul . This surname is very rare in France.
East German: habitational name from a place so named near Frankfurt on the Oder.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When John Poley was born in 1511, in Badley, Suffolk, England, his father, Edmund Poley, was 25 and his mother, Mirabelle Garneys, was 18. He married Anne Agnes Wentworth in 1541, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 20 October 1589, in his hometown, at the age of 78, and was buried in Badley, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Poley est né en 1511, à Badley, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Poley, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mirabelle Garneys, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Anne Agnes Wentworth en 1541, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 20 octobre 1589, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Badley, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Poley 1511 in Badley, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund Poley 25 und seine Mutter Mirabelle Garneys 18. Er hat 1541 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Anne Agnes Wentworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Oktober 1589 im Alter von 78 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Badley, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Poley è nato il nel 1511 nel Badley, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Edmund Poley, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mirabelle Garneys, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Anne Agnes Wentworth nel 1541, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 20 ottobre 1589, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Badley, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Poleyが1511年にイギリススフォルクバドリーで生まれた時，父Edmund Poleyは25歳，母Mirabelle Garneysは18歳であった。1541年にイギリスイギリススフォルクでAnne Agnes Wentworthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1589年10月20日に故郷で78歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクバドリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Poley(이)가 1511년 영국 수폴크 바들리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Poley(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mirabelle Garneys(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1541년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 Anne Agnes Wentworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1589년 10월 20일 그의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 바들리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Poley nasceu em 1511, em Badley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Poley, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mirabelle Garneys, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Agnes Wentworth em 1541, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de outubro de 1589, em sua cidade natal, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Badley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Poley родился в 1511 г. в местности бадли, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Poley было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mirabelle Garneys, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Agnes Wentworth в 1541 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 20 октября 1589 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности бадли, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Poley nació en 1511, en Badley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Poley, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mirabelle Garneys, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Anne Agnes Wentworth en 1541, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 20 de octubre de 1589, en su pueblo, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Badley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
